title: Fourth-party concentration risk — shared critical dependencies across vendors
exclusions: Does NOT rate the fourth-party itself; focuses on concentration score only.
evidence: Monthly 'fourth_party_concentration' writes concentration scores to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: DORA Art.28 requires institutions to assess concentration risk in ICT outsourcing; the UC provides the quantitative foundation.

controlTest.positiveScenario: When a fourth-party is a critical dependency for ≥3 high-criticality vendors, the UC fires with the fourth-party id and the concentration score (>0.6).
controlTest.negativeScenario: Fourth-parties with lower concentration OR lower upstream criticality do NOT fire.

spl:
| inputlookup vendor_dependency_graph.csv
| where vendor_criticality IN ("critical","high")
| stats dc(vendor) as dependent_count, values(vendor) as vendors by fourth_party
| eval concentration_score=min(dependent_count/3,1)
| where concentration_score>=0.6
| table fourth_party dependent_count vendors concentration_score
description: Computes a monthly concentration score for each fourth-party dependency across the vendor graph. Surfaces candidates for additional due-diligence or contractual controls.
value: Addresses the DORA supervisory-priorities theme that regulators ask 'who do your vendors depend on?'.
implementation: (1) Maintain vendor_dependency_graph.csv enriched monthly; (2) run monthly; (3) surface to risk committee.
visualization: Network graph of concentration, bar chart of fourth-parties by score, single-value 'fourth-parties above concentration threshold'.
knownFalsePositives: Depth-1 dependencies where the upstream vendor has alternate providers should be discounted (substitutability>0).
